key_quotes: Housing on campus at UNC is completely scarce
Do you go here? I've literally had friends forced off campus because of how tight on-campus housing is rn
Just looked up this complex (now called Crosstown at Chapel Hill) and its minimum $1100 for a 1 bedroom 1 bath. And that's the 'special offer'
key_points: On-campus housing is scarce and highly competitive for upperclassmen
Missing the priority deadline severely limits freshman housing options
Off-campus rents have skyrocketed far beyond levels from a decade ago

key_quotes: Greek life is (intentionally) meant to be for rich kids
Don't forget the extra inevitable expenses, not just rushing fees. It's the head-to-toe outfits, the rush bag, mixers, semi-formals
Parties are going to be the downfall of all colleges. Stopping 18-22 year olds from getting together after they have been in lockdown for months seems impossible
key_points: Greek life is expensive and largely inaccessible to financial aid students
COVID severely disrupted social development for multiple student cohorts
Some students find UNC underwhelming socially; others genuinely love it

key_quotes: I ended the semester with around 90 swipes left
Look into the Agora at Granville. Much more affordable and better food
They had a sign up last week that said '48 hours ago we were told via email that our store will be closing with no transition to another store or severance pay'
key_points: Meal plan swipes go to waste due to inflexible system design
Agora at Granville offers better food at lower cost than dining halls
Joe Van Gogh cafe closure was mishandled with no worker transition plan

key_quotes: I was extremely depressed and feeling suicidal for a good majority of the semester
Mental health awareness was always a priority in my SGA career
Maybe consider having our already scheduled days off count for something. We just had 'fall break', 2 whole days off school, and many of us had more stress
key_points: Students have reported depression and suicidal feelings during first semester
COVID transitions caused significant emotional and academic stress campuswide
SGA is actively pushing for better resources and meaningful mental health breaks

key_quotes: Just looked at my aid offer, how am I supposed to afford 30k a year
Not really. There are almost no scholarships at UNC. I am in honors and don't get any money
I get slightly different aid but I usually get around 3-4 thousand back a semester
key_points: Carolina Covenant offers strong support for low-income students
Middle-class families face sticker shock at roughly $30k per year
Right aid combinations can yield $3,000–$6,000 refund checks per semester

key_quotes: Mark Peifer is the best + my favorite biology professor at UNC
I'm just about to finish my sophomore year with a major in biology. However in these past two years all I have been getting are C's in the weed out biology and chemistry classes
I heard I have to re-apply to actually major in computer science, right now I'm 'intended'
key_points: Pre-med weed-out culture is intense with many students averaging Cs
Academic quality is strong but varies widely by department and instructor
CS major requires re-application to declare, surprising many transfer students

key_quotes: The UNC school system KNEW this was going to happen and just went ahead and fucked over thousands of students
They had a sign up that said '48 hours ago we were told via email that our store will be closing with no transition'
They're already planning to expand admissions over the course of several years but as it stands right now, they literally don't have the room to add more students
key_points: COVID handling was poor — UNC went online just one week in
Joe Van Gogh closure gave workers zero notice or transition support
Transfer credit appeals and housing bureaucracy are frustratingly slow processes

key_quotes: Chapel Hill is a safe town. That extends to the buses
Buses are free in town and then it takes me 20 min to campus
Until 2006, in order to graduate from UNC-Chapel Hill, students had to prove they knew how to swim
key_points: Chapel Hill is safe, walkable, and served by free campus buses
Franklin Street offers solid dining, coffee, and entertainment nearby
Beautiful historic campus faces pressure from rapid enrollment growth

key_quotes: Kenan-Flagler (UNC's business school) is elite
It's never too early [to pursue research]; there are many instances where freshman get research opportunities lined up for the fall
NC admission statistics: Duke: 9%, UNC-Chapel Hill: 29%... NC State's engineering program is excellent
key_points: Kenan-Flagler Business School is elite and a strong career launchpad
CS and STEM grads rely more on skills and networking than diploma
Research opportunities are available to freshmen who proactively contact professors

key_quotes: I pay 2k lol
me when i drop 70k a year for a state school
The price calculator estimated it to be half of what it's actually saying
key_points: In-state students can pay as little as $2,000 with strong aid
Out-of-state students face $30k+ with very limited merit aid available
Rigid meal plans and tight housing reduce overall value for many

key_quotes: I worked all throughout high school to get into this prestigious college
2026 grad - everyone i know had really low expectations but we were all really blown away by the speech
I am currently a freshman at UNC and so far I do not like it... I worked all throughout high school to get into this prestigious college
key_points: Students who find their groove genuinely love UNC Chapel Hill
Campus is academically competitive, socially diverse, and beautifully situated
Enrollment growth and administrative stumbles create real friction for students

key_quotes: UNC-Chapel Hill fall semester going online amid COVID-19 outbreaks, one week into classes
I've literally had friends forced off campus because of how tight on-campus housing is rn
They're already planning to expand admissions but as it stands right now, they literally don't have the room to add more students
key_points: COVID policy: UNC sent students home just one week into fall 2020
Housing: Business school admits still struggle to secure preferred on-campus housing
Growth: 85,000 applicants for a campus with no room left to expand

key_quotes: Consider adding APPL101 or APPL110 to your degree. Whatever your major is, you can be a UNC graduate who is knowledgeable about prototyping or have good programming and modeling skills
From my experience [at Project Uplift Plus], we did a lot of tours around professional schools like med, dental, law, pharmacy
Look into the Agora at Granville. Much more affordable and better food. Only thing is you'll have to walk 'off campus'. It's literally right at the edge
key_points: APPL101 and APPL110 teach practical engineering and coding skills for all majors
Agora at Granville offers better, cheaper food but gets no official promotion
Project Uplift Plus is a low-profile summer program with strong alumni reviews
